title: Playground Teaching Browser
concept: A browser-chrome redesign for educators running workshops: Playgrounds are tabs, the WordPress path is an address bar, save state is visible in the chrome, and creation, saved management, Site Manager tools, gallery browsing, import, and export live in contextual side drawers.
focus: contextual side drawers instead of modal stacks
light gray workspace with green status accents
flows: Create a Playground from vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, zip import, or featured Blueprint shortcuts from the Launch drawer.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with visible progress and persistent save state in the browser chrome.
Manage unsaved and saved Playgrounds as browser tabs and library rows with rename and delete actions.
Use Site Manager as a contextual drawer with settings, file browser, Blueprint, database, logs, and export tools while the live WordPress page remains visible.
Browse and filter all Blueprint gallery entries, then run a selected Blueprint or use featured workshop starters.
Navigate Homepage, WP Admin, and arbitrary WordPress paths from the browser address bar.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
understand destructive reset and stored Save & Reload behavior
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
